symptom_query: my cost estimate for an eval run was far off from what actually ran
target: derive estimates from real per-cell/per-trial costs from a prior result JSON, not model-tier rules of thumb

symptom_query: I restructured the payload or optimized the assumed bottleneck but cost or time barely improved
target: verify where the bulk actually lives (instrument/measure) before acting on an efficiency lever; assumed location is often wrong

symptom_query: I cannot find a targ target to rebuild or install the engram binary
target: install with 'go install ./cmd/engram'; there is no 'targ build' target — targ covers test/lint/check only

symptom_query: my lazy or on-demand retrieval shipped but the agent is not fetching deferred content at the right time or at all
target: measure the agent's uninstructed natural fetch behavior before shipping: verify it fetches sparing (under break-even) AND can fetch the right item unprompted when it is the sole source

symptom_query: I moved a step to async or background processing but total token usage or cost did not decrease
target: async/background relocates work off the critical path but does not eliminate it; only work truly removed counts toward a tokens/cost/time reduction

symptom_query: I increased how often a recall or gate fires but the failure rate did not drop as much as expected
target: classify failures as addressable/capture-ceiling/application first, then derive over-fire at a pinned fire-unit; ceiling failures are unreachable regardless of frequency, and over-fire multiplies cost

symptom_query: my corpus mined from session logs is dominated by one project and the patterns I found feel skewed or non-general
target: a retained store is silently biased toward the active/recent project (others age out); cross-check with a breadth source the pruning did not touch before generalizing

symptom_query: a deterministic batch job or ingest is running far longer than the input volume seems to warrant
target: diagnose by measurement: instrument and time each sub-step to find the actual bottleneck; do not guess at the cause

symptom_query: my keyword-based detector for user corrections or agent failures in transcripts has low yield or misses obvious cases
target: detect semantically — read and judge each turn in context — not by keyword; keyword matching false-negatives on paraphrased or implicit corrections

symptom_query: I used a cheap model tier for large-scale classification but I am not confident the results are accurate
target: validate the cheap tier against a hand-built gold set on a small sample before deploying at volume

symptom_query: I am using embedding similarity to detect or classify failures and getting weak or misleading signal
target: use LLM reasoning for detection and classification, not embedding retrieval — embeddings judge similarity, not whether reasoning holds

symptom_query: my tests pass for a function returning a struct with a boolean gate field but I may be missing behavioral paths
target: test both true and false paths of the binary gate field explicitly, not just the main payload content

symptom_query: conclusions I drew during a work session are not surfacing when I search engram later
target: raw session chunks and vault notes are separate spaces — conclusions only become searchable if explicitly written as vault notes via learn

symptom_query: I changed which item type ranks higher in retrieval but I am not sure the system actually improved
target: evaluate ranking changes by whether the right knowledge reaches the agent unprompted, not by which item-type outranked another

symptom_query: after simplifying or redesigning part of my system I lost reliable deterministic behavior that used to work
target: re-host existing deterministic machinery in the new design rather than substituting it away during simplification

symptom_query: I found files that were deleted outside my own actions and I am about to restore them
target: surface what changed and ask the user whether the deletion was intentional before restoring anything

symptom_query: I edited a SKILL.md but the agent behavior does not seem to have reliably improved
target: skill edits require writing-skills TDD: RED baseline run showing the failure, then edit, then GREEN verification with fresh agent — no shortcuts

symptom_query: the implementation I built from an LLM-generated spec does not match what was actually needed
target: run an explicit alignment gate before executing any LLM-generated spec — trace each requirement against the original ask and resolve gaps first

symptom_query: I changed a workflow orchestration skill and it now produces unexpected or broken behavior
target: commit a plan doc and run fresh-agent pressure tests before editing any workflow-orchestration skill

symptom_query: I am asking the user to approve a plan or design but it is buried in chat and hard to review
target: write the full synthesis to a reviewable artifact first with decisions split into user-decided vs agent-proposed, then ask for approval

symptom_query: skill I wrote for TDD or review discipline isn't changing how capable models behave
target: Capable models already know the discipline — scope the skill to ENFORCE it (gates, checks, blockers), not teach it

symptom_query: user asked for N diverse options to evaluate but I pre-filtered some before delivering
target: Always deliver all N requested options with honest per-option ratings; never prune before the user evaluates

symptom_query: recency re-ranking has no visible effect on chunk query results after wiring it in
target: applyChunkRecency + sortScoredDesc must run on scoredChunk before the resolvedItem conversion step

symptom_query: note recency decay multiplier isn't changing which notes rank highest after I wired it in
target: Add baseScore float32 to scoredCandidate; apply the multiplier inside rankCandidates before the sort step

symptom_query: linter reports struct fields as unused even though they are planned for a later task
target: Add a minimal consumer (e.g., a default-return helper) so the linter passes without nolint suppressions

symptom_query: RunQuery chunk-only integration test fails or returns unexpected results
target: QueryDeps.Scan must return nil notes (not an empty slice or real notes) for the chunk-only path to work correctly

symptom_query: adding a sidecar write side-effect to RunQuery causes locking or data-race issues
target: Add a Lock (or WriteSidecar-that-locks) field to QueryDeps; skip the sidecar write if nil (dry mode)

symptom_query: after lifting a variable from inner to outer scope one of funlen or wsl_v5 always fires no matter what I try
target: funlen and wsl_v5 have competing constraints at Go scope boundaries — the note documents the specific tension that makes both unsatisfiable with a plain var lift

symptom_query: recency eval always scores the planted recent chunk perfectly regardless of halfLife or floor settings
target: Homogeneous old distractors make the recency band trivially dominant; spread distractor ages across realistic range and add a mid-age probe

symptom_query: I need a value from inside an if-block at an outer call site but declaring a var at outer scope breaks wsl_v5 or funlen
target: Inline the value expression directly at the outer call site instead of declaring a var in the outer scope

symptom_query: my recency half-life eval shows no discrimination between arms even though I planted a recent chunk
target: uniform old distractor pool collapses recency signal; spread distractors across realistic age tiers (e.g. 0.5d, 2d, 5d, 15d, 60d)

symptom_query: user says my time-based defaults feel wrong or mismatched to how they actually work
target: ask the user's actual work cadence/usage rhythm before setting time-based defaults; never assume a timescale

symptom_query: my age-in-days test assertions fail with unexpected fractional or off-by-one values when parsing YYYY-MM-DD date strings
target: time.Parse("2006-01-02") produces midnight UTC; computed age-in-days needs noon arithmetic to avoid fractional-day boundary errors

symptom_query: lint fails with unused field error on a struct field I added for a future implementation phase
target: add a test accessor or export the field; unexported forward-declared fields must be referenced somewhere to satisfy the linter

symptom_query: linter or modernize tool flags strings.Split used in a range-for loop in a Go 1.24+ project
target: replace strings.Split(s, sep) in range-for with strings.SplitSeq(s, sep) on Go 1.24+

symptom_query: adding a new time-based field to the sidecar struct causes unnecessary re-embedding on every run
target: tag the field omitempty and exclude it from ContentHash; time-varying fields in ContentHash trigger re-embed on every write

symptom_query: recency activation gate is incorrectly excluding candidates that should pass the cutoff threshold
target: evaluate the cutoff on the base score before applying decay, not on the already-decayed score

symptom_query: CLI command passes all unit tests but fails with path or cwd errors when run with real file path arguments
target: in-memory path tests and no-arg no-ops hide cwd/vault path-resolution bugs; run the installed binary with real args from a non-data-dir cwd before declaring done

symptom_query: output is missing its trailing newline after stripping a trailing section with bytes.Split and bytes.Join
target: bytes.Join does not restore the trailing newline that bytes.Split consumed; check and append it explicitly after joining

symptom_query: nilaway linter fails on code that indexes into the result of bytes.Split
target: add `if lines == nil { return body }` guard immediately after bytes.Split, before any indexing

symptom_query: a review gate or reviewer is blocking my change saying it reverses or violates a locked design decision
target: verify the specific decision's actual scope against the real code change before accepting the block — the reversal claim may be out of scope

symptom_query: my design or implementation turned out wrong because docs or memory described a mechanism that had already been removed
target: after any major removal, scrub all docs, memory notes, and diagrams that name the old path in the same effort — stale docs silently corrupt downstream designs

symptom_query: I am reasoning about or designing against how a system mechanism works without having checked the actual current code
target: trace the real call chain from the entry point in the current codebase; docs and memory are hints to verify, not ground truth

symptom_query: I finished removing a code path or flag but docs and memory notes still describe it as if it exists
target: a removal is not done until every doc, memory note, glossary entry, and diagram naming the old path is updated or deleted in the same effort

symptom_query: after removing or renaming a flag or subcommand, test or eval harnesses are broken or behaving unexpectedly
target: treat test/eval harnesses like production consumers — verify them explicitly after any cross-cutting flag/API removal or rename

symptom_query: my TDD RED baseline is not failing in the way the design assumed — it passes or shows a different failure mode
target: surface the measured reality to the user with numbers; reframe the spec to match observation — never engineer the fixture to reproduce the assumed failure mode

symptom_query: a reviewer is blocking my change with a claim that seems wrong, overstated, or not supported by what I see in the code
target: verify the reviewer's blocking claim against the actual code/runtime the same way you'd verify any other assertion — treat reviewer claims as untested hypotheses

symptom_query: considering adding graph expansion or reranking to improve recall coverage
target: Validate any retrieval improvement layer through the recall eval before building it — don't assume it helps.

symptom_query: proposing synthesis or multi-hop scaffolding to improve the engram recall flow
target: Engram's value is memory content, not reasoning scaffolding — validate the memory contribution separately from any scaffold's reasoning contribution.

symptom_query: agent already derives synthesis conclusions in-session so the synthesis feature seems redundant
target: In-session reasoning and cross-session persistence are different axes — an agent can derive a conclusion in-session yet fail to recall it next session; don't conflate them.

symptom_query: synthesis eval is passing but unclear whether it actually tests real synthesis
target: Synthesis eval fixtures must require emergent, non-literal conclusions the agent cannot find verbatim in any single note — otherwise literal retrieval masquerades as synthesis.

symptom_query: agent reasoned out conclusions from recalled notes; unsure whether to persist them to vault
target: Persist sound reasoned conclusions as synthesis notes (recall Step 4) for inspection — makes the reasoning chain reviewable cross-session, not accuracy-guaranteed.

symptom_query: trying to cut memory-augmented agent cost by reducing recall payload size
target: Recall payload is not the cost bottleneck; the agent's build/task loop dominates — target procedure overhead (model tier, fewer steps, async learn), not payload.

symptom_query: want to delegate recall or retrieval work to a cheaper model to save money
target: Split the skill at its judgment seam — strong host keeps reasoning that needs its context; delegate only large autonomous blocks to a cheaper subskill, not bespoke infra.

symptom_query: adjusting content-budget in query.go to reduce cost or token usage
target: Content-budget default is baked to 15 (0 resolves to 15, negative = unlimited); it was not the cost bottleneck — the build/task loop dominates.

symptom_query: planning to split recall skill across strong and cheap models for cost savings
target: Do not split a skill across models for cost unless the delegated part is a large, autonomous sub-task — the added complexity isn't worth it otherwise.

symptom_query: have eval results and want to recommend a new optimization lever
target: Before recommending any lever post-eval, actively check whether it was already tried and rejected in this session — re-proposing closed levers is a known failure mode.

symptom_query: previously-closed approach is being re-proposed as fresh even after running recall
target: The miss is structural — recall doesn't surface the lesson under the re-proposal query because the note isn't stored/triggered under that signal; fix note wording or trigger type, not retrieval tuning.

symptom_query: agent gives correct answers in recall eval but unclear if recall actually contributed
target: Pair the end metric with a diagnostic sub-metric checking whether the disproving note was retrieved/cited — otherwise retrieval failure and synthesis failure look identical.

symptom_query: cost and time breakdown across recall phases is confusing or mixing different axes
target: Keep time and dollar axes separate and label each; report the marginal premium (why memory costs more than no-memory) separately from absolute phase cost.

symptom_query: building a simple isolated eval to reproduce the anti-amnesia miss but it keeps passing
target: Behavioral lever-recheck misses are emergent from rich multi-session context and are not reproducible in a single-session toy fixture — the behavioral failure requires realistic session depth.

symptom_query: my eval fixture is too small to reproduce a note buried among many not surfaced by retrieval
target: put a stub binary on PATH that fakes the buried-at-scale condition rather than building a large real fixture

symptom_query: I am about to recommend or re-scope something right after the user explicitly told me what to do
target: before recommending, reconcile against the most recent explicit user instruction; don't override without flagging the conflict

symptom_query: my test prompt for the task-displacement failure is not catching the behavior in isolation
target: the 'please' lean instruction amplifies displacement; design anti-sycophantic challenge instructions targeting that specific pathway

symptom_query: I wrote an instruction to stop an agent from substituting prerequisite work but it still displaces the original task
target: name the exact self-deception in the instruction (e.g. 'substituting more rigorous prep') and require explicit user confirmation before any substitution

symptom_query: I have been executing a chain of locally reasonable steps and I am not sure they still serve the original stated goal
target: periodically stop and re-anchor: restate the goal, revalidate current plan against it with fresh measurements rather than carrying forward prior conclusions

symptom_query: I am optimizing engram recall or learn operation speed but the overall system value is not improving
target: engram's net value is in avoiding rebuild waste on idiosyncratic builds, not in recall/learn op speed; focus effort on build-side payoff

symptom_query: I concluded that a capability improvement is fundamentally impossible but my reasoning spanned multiple axes at once
target: decompose structural vs contingent impossibility per axis before asserting 'cannot'; axes may be independently achievable even when the combined conclusion looks negative

symptom_query: the harness shows recall_s around 350s and I am attributing that cost entirely to the recall step
target: 'recall_s' is round-1 duration (recall + the full paging/build step), not recall alone; actual recall is ~186s and step-2 paging dominates

symptom_query: a conclusion I built on a named metric or log field turned out wrong after the fact
target: before concluding from any named metric/field/log value, trace the code to verify what it actually captures — names are hints, not guarantees

symptom_query: memory-assisted builds are consistently coming out slower and more expensive than cold baseline builds
target: on small easy builds, warm is structurally slower and costlier; memory value only appears on builds with idiosyncratic content that cold opus cannot one-shot

symptom_query: I am about to report a positive eval finding that confirms the hoped-for result
target: adversarially verify every favorable nuance before reporting — favorable readings frequently flip under scrutiny; verify first, then report

symptom_query: cold opus is solving build traps I expected to require memory and my eval fixtures are not producing the failures I predicted
target: cold failure is empirical, not predictable; pilot each gotcha individually to confirm it actually fools cold opus before including it in an eval

symptom_query: my cheap reproducible build eval shows memory has no advantage and I am considering concluding memory is not worth it
target: cheap isolated build-trap evals structurally cannot demonstrate memory's behavioral value; showing that value requires idiosyncratic rich session context

symptom_query: I am not sure which tasks or use cases engram memory measurably improves versus hurts
target: memory's measured wins are only on idiosyncratic content (C3 convention traps, C6 abduction); it is neutral-to-negative on standard tasks — use that as the decision filter
